channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump signs order instructing DOGE to massively cut federal workforce
item.description: President Donald Trump expected to sign an executive order Tuesday instructing the Department of Government Efficiency to plan for cuts to federal workforce staffing numbers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia releases imprisoned American Marc Fogel in what US calls a step toward the end of Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Marc Fogel, an American teacher who was deemed wrongfully detained by Russia, has been released in what the White House described as a diplomatic thaw that could advance negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eric Adams reiterates innocence amid DOJ order
item.description: The Justice Department ordered federal prosecutors to drop corruption charges against the New York City mayor.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly half of NY voters don’t want Mayor Adams’ charges dropped: poll
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Nearly half of New Yorkers oppose Mayor Eric Adams’ charges being dropped, according to a recent PIX11 poll.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Murphy looks to be guiding force for Democrats in new Trump era
item.description: Sen. Chris Murphy (D-Conn.) is taking a front-and-center role in the efforts to push Democrats toward becoming the party of economic populism and to challenge President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s proposal for Gaza entangles Arab allies
item.description: President Trump’s proposal to permanently resettle Palestinians from the Gaza Strip is complicating relationships with allies in the Arab world.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth renames North Carolina military base Fort Roland L. Bragg
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has signed an order restoring the name of a storied special operations forces base back to Fort Bragg.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Justice Department official orders prosecutors to drop charges against New York Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Justice Department on Monday ordered federal prosecutors to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams, arguing in a remarkable departure from long-standing norms that the case was interfering with the mayor’s ability to aid the president’s crackdown on illega
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DHS seeks to deputize IRS agents to help with deportation efforts
item.description: Trump has reportedly been increasingly frustrated with the number of immigration arrests since returning to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: An Ely group agrees on the value of the Boundary Waters
item.description: Whether they’re conservative or liberal, people in Ely seem to agree on one thing: they love the Boundary Waters. What they can’t seem to agree on, though, is whether copper-nickel mining would endanger those beloved lakes.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: After meeting with Trump, Jordan's king says his country opposes displacing Palestinians in Gaza
item.description: President Donald Trump has hosted Jordan’s King Abdullah II at the White House and has renewed his insistence that Gaza could somehow be emptied of all residents, controlled by the U.S. and redeveloped as a tourist area.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Egg price bill advances at Nevada Legislature; AB171 could increase supply in 30 days
item.description: A bill aimed at reducing the skyrocketing price of eggs in Nevada advanced on Monday, but the legislative process will take some time even if there's no opposition.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Battleground Florida: Officials discuss tariffs, LGBTQ policies
item.description: University of Tampa Economics Professor Michael Coon explained the purpose of tariffs and the impact they will likely have on businesses and families in Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis moves forward on proposal to shift violence prevention programs to the county
item.description: After a long and sometimes heated discussion Monday afternoon, the Minneapolis City Council agreed to move forward with a controversial proposal to shift around $1.2 million to Hennepin County so that it can temporarily take over the management of two city violence prevention programs.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: The Memo: Trump, allies ramp up attacks on courts
item.description: Monday brought another legal setback for President Trump and his administration — and there is every indication it will add more fuel to the fire of MAGA grievances against the judiciary. The issue was federal spending and the new president’s desire to freeze huge swathes of it. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard, Trump's pick to oversee US spy agencies, advances to Senate confirmation vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Tulsi Gabbard moved a step closer to being the next director of national intelligence Monday after the U.S. Senate advanced her nomination to a final vote planned for later this week.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Tulsi Gabbard nomination to be nation's intelligence chief
item.description: The Senate voted largely along party lines Monday evening to advance Tulsi Gabbard's nomination to serve as President Trump’s director of national intelligence (DNI), putting her on a glide path to confirmation later this week.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scientists warn Trump's medical research cuts endanger patients as judge blocks the move for now
item.description: Scientists warn the Trump administration’s drastic cuts for medical research endanger patients, will cost thousands of jobs – and threaten America’s standing as a world leader in science and innovation.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New felony count added to charges against Sen. Nicole Mitchell for April 2024 incident
item.description: Becker County Attorney Brian McDonald added a second burglary charge Monday against state Sen. Nicole Mitchell, DFL-Woodbury, stemming from an incident in April 2024 in Detroit Lakes. Both charges are felonies.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Mathew Holding Eagle III, MPR News

item.title: Congress now has a DOGE Caucus. Here's how its co-chair says it will work
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Rep. Pete Sessions, co-chair of the House DOGE Caucus, on how he plans to work with the Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly, Kai McNamee, and Justine Kenin, MPR News

item.title: Republicans put fraud, measures to stop it in spotlight as they take Minnesota House reins
item.description: An initial hearing of a fraud prevention committee in the Minnesota House was a first order of business for Republicans with their newly recognized chamber control.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Trump administration has stopped work at the CFPB. Here's what the agency does
item.description: It's the main U.S. agency tasked with overseeing the financial products and services used by everyday Americans. The CFPB's new head has closed its offices and told staff to stay home.
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez, MPR News

item.title: U.S. judge keeps his block on Trump federal buyout plan in place for now
item.description: A U.S. judge kept his block on President Donald Trump’s buyout plan for federal employees in place on Monday and said he would consider imposing it for a longer period of time.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Trump pardons ex-Illinois Gov. Rod Blagojevich 5 years after commuting his sentence
item.description: Former Illinois Gov. Rod Blagojevich says he’s “profoundly grateful” after President Donald Trump pardoned him on political corruption charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump: Palestinians wouldn't have right to return to developed Gaza
item.description: "No, they wouldn't, because they're going to have much better housing. Much better," Trump told Fox News's Bret Baier in an interview.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's 'Fork in the Road' resignation offer for federal workers is in judge's hands
item.description: After a hearing on Monday, a federal judge in Boston extended a stay on the deadline for federal employees to accept the Trump administration's resignation offer while he considers the arguments.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirmation process for Trump nominees: How it works
item.description: Several nominees for Cabinet roles with President Donald Trump's administration still must be confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Palestinian refugees in Syria have a message for Gazans: Don’t leave your land
item.description: Palestinians fled the 1948 Mideast War and took refuge in neighboring Syria. After 77 years, they’re still waiting to go back. They are telling Palestinians in Gaza to stay put.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre and Jawad Rizkallah, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s two cents: President orders U.S. Treasury to ditch the penny to save money
item.description: President Donald Trump says he has directed the Treasury Department to stop minting new pennies, citing the rising cost of producing the one-cent coin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Vance proves pivotal in pushing through controversial Trump nominees
item.description: Vice President Vance has quickly become President Trump's liaison to the Senate, which has paid dividends as the GOP-led body moves to confirm even Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: A timeline of US-China tit-for-tat tariffs since Trump's first term
item.description: China’s tit-for-tat duties on U.S. imports took effect Monday, just hours after U.S. President Donald Trump announced that he wants to slap new duties on all steel and aluminum imports to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, Fox 5
